reasoning: The question asks whether any of the listed candidates (Gnangbo Kacou, Laurent Gbagbo, Pascal Affi N'Guessan, Jean-Louis Billon, or Simone Gbagbo) will be disqualified, removed from the electoral list, or otherwise become legally ineligible to run in the 2025 Ivorian presidential election before July 1, 2025. Search results explicitly confirm that former President Laurent Gbagbo was excluded from the provisional electoral list published by the Independent Electoral Commission (CEI) in March 2025. His exclusion stems from a 2018 Ivorian court conviction sentencing him to 20 years in prison concerning the 'BCEAO robbery,' which strips him of his civic rights. The Ivorian government has refused calls (including from the African Court) to reinstate him. Because Laurent Gbagbo is specifically named in the resolution criteria and is currently both legally ineligible to run and removed from the electoral list as of the cutoff date, the criteria for a YES resolution are securely met. (Additionally, another major opposition figure, Tidjane Thiam, was also disqualified in April 2025, but his exclusion is immaterial to the resolution since Gbagbo's disqualification alone satisfies the 'any of these candidates' condition).
